claim: Vibe coding (forgetting the code exists) becomes viable in production when task length AI can handle doubles every 7 months; preparing for a day's or week's worth of AI work requires different workflows
actionable: Plan now for an exponential curve: expect AI to handle day-long tasks in 1-2 years; design verification systems (tests, acceptance criteria) that work without reading all generated code.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude

claim: Managing unread code is solvable like a CTO reviewing expert work: use acceptance tests, stress tests, and human-verifiable inputs/outputs instead of reading all code
actionable: Design systems with verifiable checkpoints (tests pass, inputs/outputs correct) even if you don't read the implementation; focus on outcome verification, not code review.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude

claim: Focus AI-generated code on leaf nodes (features nothing depends on) rather than core architecture; tech debt in leaf nodes won't propagate like it would in trunks/branches
actionable: Let Claude vibe-code features and helpers; keep humans deeply involved in architecture, shared utilities, and extensibility points where changes cascade.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude

claim: Ask 'what can you do for Claude' not 'what can Claude do for you': spend 15-20 minutes collecting guidance (codebase tour, requirements, patterns) before letting Claude work
actionable: Act as Claude's product manager: explore the codebase together, create a plan document with files to change and patterns to follow, then hand off the artifact to Claude.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude

claim: Anthropic merged 22,000 lines of production RL code heavily written by Claude by focusing on leaf nodes, human review of core parts, and designing verifiable stress tests
actionable: When doing large AI-generated changes, invest in test infrastructure and accept that only critical components need detailed human review if inputs/outputs are verifiable.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude

claim: Tech debt is the one thing hard to validate without reading code; it's the main reason to constrain AI code generation to leaf nodes and avoid it in foundational layers
actionable: Until models improve, avoid letting Claude generate foundational code (shared utilities, core architecture) where tech debt would block future work; focus on features.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude

claim: Being lazy with vibe coding leads to hallucinations and security failures; vibe coding responsibly requires deep understanding of what questions to ask Claude
actionable: Don't fully automate with Claude unless you understand security implications; vet dangerous operations (payments, API calls) and guide Claude through risky decisions.
source_title: Vibe coding in prod | Code w/ Claude
